title: Wire Object
description: Straight or curved line segment in a PCB board, schematic sheet, symbol, or package.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: arc
properties.description: Arc geometry when wire is curved; null for straight wires (curve=0).

properties.name: cap
properties.description: Shape applied to the wire endpoints (flat or round cap).

properties.name: curve
properties.description: Arc curvature in degrees; 0 for straight lines.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: layer
properties.description: Layer number (1-based index into the layer stack).

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: style
properties.description: Dash pattern applied to this wire.

properties.name: width
properties.description: Stroke width of this wire, in internal units.

properties.name: x1
properties.description: X coordinate of the start point, in internal units.

properties.name: x2
properties.description: X coordinate of the end point, in internal units.

properties.name: y1
properties.description: Y coordinate of the start point, in internal units.

properties.name: y2
properties.description: Y coordinate of the end point, in internal units.
